title: Inhibitor-free DNA extraction from unsorted bulk samples
author: Dominik Buchner

steps_raw.instruction: Prepare one 50 mL centrifuge tube per sample with 7.5 g of garnet beads (0.3 mm diameter) and 7.5 g of zirconia beads (2 mm diameter).
Add up to 20 mL, bulk sample in ethanol to the tube. Tube with up to 20 mL of bulk sample in ethanol

Add 16.2 mL, universal lysis buffer and 1.5 mL, of Proteinase K stock solution Vortex shortly. The universal lysis buffer tends to precipitate at room temperature. Heat the buffer to ~ 70 °C until it is clear again.
Place the samples on a Vortex adapter (e.g. Qiagen) and vortex at maximum speed for 00:10:00 .
2500 x g, 20 °C, 3 minutes . Transfer the supernatant to a new tube. For the large volume protocol, the samples can be carefully poured instead of being pipetted.
Add 5 mL, ammonium acetate buffer , vortex shortly, and incubate at 4 °C for 00:05:00 .
2500 x g, 20 °C, 4 minutes . Transfer the supernatant to a new tube avoiding the pellet. The solution may still be colored, depending on the input material.
Add 4 mL, of inhibitor removal buffer . A precipitate may form. Vortex shortly, incubate at 4 °C for 00:05:00 .
2500 x g, 20 °C, 4 minutes . The solution will clear up. Avoiding the pellet, transfer up to 15 mL to a new tube.
Add 30 mL, DNA binding buffer . Vortex or invert to mix.
Add the mixture to a maxi spin column (e.g. Epoch Life Science) in a 50 mL centrifuge tube.
2500 x g, 20 °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through. Repeat once to bind the complete sample volume.
Add 10 mL, wash buffer . 2500 x g, 20 °C, 5 minutes to wash and dry the column.
Transfer the column to a new tube. Add 1 mL, elution buffer . Incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature .
2500 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute to elute the DNA. DNA eluate should be completely colorless and ready to go for downstream analysis.
Prepare one 2 mL centrifuge tube per sample with 750 mg of garnet beads (0.3 mm diameter) and 750 mg of zirconia beads (2 mm diameter).
Add 1000 µL of unsorted bulk sample. 4000 x g, 20 °C, 3 minutes and remove as much of the ethanol as possible without disturbing the pellet.
Add 900 µL, universal lysis buffer and 100 µL, Proteinase K . Vortex shortly.

Place the samples in a bead-beater (e.g. BioSpec Products Mini Bead-beater) and bead-beat at maximum speed for 00:02:00 . If no bead-beater is available this step can be skipped, but the yield might decrease.
10000 x g, 20 °C, 3 minutes . Transfer 600 µL of the supernatant to a new tube. If you want to extract technical replicates, this is the right time to split your samples into 2x 300 µL .
Add 250 µL, ammonium acetate buffer , vortex shortly, and incubate at 4 °C for 00:05:00 .
10000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute . Transfer the supernatant to a new tube.
Add 200 µL, of inhibitor removal buffer . A precipitate may form. Vortex shortly, incubate at 4 °C for 00:05:00 .
10000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute . Transfer 600 µL of the supernatant to a new tube.

Add 1200 µL, DNA binding buffer . Vortex to mix. Depending on the amount of lysate and / or technical replication the amount of binding buffer differs. General rule: The amount of binding buffer should be at least twice the amount of lysate / sample material.
Load 650 µL of the mixture to a mini spin column (e.g. Epoch Life Science).
10000 x g, 20 °C, 30 seconds . Discard the flow-through. Repeat two times to bind the complete sample volume.
Add 500 µL, wash buffer . 10000 x g, 20 °C, 30 seconds to wash the column. Discard the flow-through.
10000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute to dry the column. Transfer the spin column to a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

Add 50 µL, elution buffer . Incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature . The amount of elution buffer is variable and can range from 30 to 200 µL depending if concentrated sample or more volume is required.
10000 x g, 20 °C, 1 minute to elute the DNA. DNA eluate should be completely colorless and ready to go for downstream analysis.
4000 x g, Room temperature, 3 minutes to pellet the material. Decant as much ethanol as possible. After centrifugation the material is pelleted After decanting the ethanol.
1400 rpm, 56 °C, 30 minutes to fully lyse the material.
Place the samples on a Thermonlock and incubate for at least 00:30:00 at 56 °C and at maximum RPM
